title: Rc 1
description: Rc<T> (Reference Counting) enables multiple ownership of the same data in single-threaded scenarios.
instructions: Use Rc to share data between multiple owners.
hints: Use Rc::clone(&rc) to create a new reference to the same data.
Rc::clone doesn't deep-copy the data - it increments the reference count.
When an Rc is dropped, the count decreases. Data is freed when count reaches 0.
explanation: Rc<T> (Reference Counting) allows multiple parts of your program to read the same data. `Rc::clone()` is cheap - it just increments a counter. When each Rc is dropped, the counter decrements. When the last Rc is dropped (count reaches 0), the data is cleaned up. Rc is for single-threaded use only; for multi-threaded code, use Arc instead.
